reasons: Baxia reduces the value of Bane's Ale healing and extended-trade sustain.
Baxia can frontline into Bane so the team can punish him before he resets with healing.

proof.summary: Bane wants to survive trades through Ale healing, while Baxia reduces healing and regeneration value so Bane has less time to reset the fight.
worksBestWhen: Baxia tags Bane before Bane can disengage and heal safely.
Allies focus Bane during the anti-heal window.
failureCases: Bane keeps distance and pokes before Baxia reaches him.
Bane disengages before the team can follow up on Baxia's pressure.

reasons: Valir can push Bane away and slow his approach before Bane reaches a strong Deadly Catch angle.
Bane has to walk into many fights, so Valir's disengage makes his setup less reliable.

proof.summary: Bane wants to move forward for poke, sustain, and Deadly Catch pressure, while Valir can slow and knock him back before he reaches the backline.
worksBestWhen: Valir holds knockback for Bane's real forward movement.
Valir positions outside Bane's ultimate setup angle.
failureCases: Bane catches Valir with Deadly Catch before Valir can disengage.
Valir wastes knockback on a lower-priority target.

reasons: Dyrroth can punish Bane in close-range fights with burst and defense reduction.
This works best when Dyrroth forces the fight before Bane gets repeated Ale sustain.

proof.summary: Bane wants time to heal and extend trades, while Dyrroth's defense-reduction burst can shorten the fight before Bane's sustain matters.
worksBestWhen: Dyrroth engages after Bane uses Ale or misses poke.
Dyrroth has enough damage backup to finish Bane quickly.
failureCases: Bane pokes Dyrroth down before the all-in.
Dyrroth dives into Deadly Catch without follow-up.

reasons: Karrie can pressure durable fighters with repeated tank-shred damage.
Bane's sustain is less valuable when Karrie has enough time and protection to keep attacking him.

proof.summary: Bane wants to function as a durable sustained fighter, while Karrie can wear him down with repeated attacks when protected.
worksBestWhen: Karrie has frontline protection from Bane's engage.
Karrie can keep distance and attack through the fight.
failureCases: Bane reaches Karrie with Deadly Catch setup.
Karrie is forced to retreat before stacking damage.

reasons: Lunox can punish Bane's durability with burst and penetration-style magic damage.
Her mobility and burst timing help her avoid long trades where Bane's sustain becomes more valuable.

proof.summary: Bane wants extended fights where his healing has value, while Lunox can use burst windows to punish him before he stabilizes.
worksBestWhen: Lunox enters after Bane uses key control or sustain.
Lunox has enough damage to force Bane low quickly.
failureCases: Bane catches Lunox before she can reposition.
Lunox commits without enough damage or escape timing.
